packs.title: Diverse Image Ad Archetypes
packs.summary: Twenty-five reusable image-ad families for broad commercial exploration across UGC, surreal 3D, premium still life, demo, comparison, retail, ecommerce, OOH, proof, launch, editorial, and social-native formats.
packs.whenToUse: Use when the user wants a wide range of very different ad directions for a supplied subject before narrowing to scenes, style routes, or final production assets.
requiredInputs: product, service, venue, or offer facts to preserve
channel or aspect ratio if known
CTA or contact detail if text is requested

placeholders.name: SUBJECT_REFERENCE
placeholders.description: Uploaded or supplied subject to preserve exactly.

placeholders.name: SUBJECT_KIND
placeholders.description: Product, service, venue, experience, app, offer, or business asset.

placeholders.name: PRIMARY_BENEFIT
placeholders.description: The main approved benefit or buying cue.

placeholders.name: BRAND_PALETTE
placeholders.description: Core colors and material cues to echo without overmatching.

placeholders.name: HEADLINE
placeholders.description: Exact supplied headline, or no headline when not supplied.

placeholders.name: CTA_OR_CONTACT
placeholders.description: Exact supplied CTA or contact detail, or no CTA when not supplied.

placeholders.name: AVOID
placeholders.description: Subject-specific risks, unsupported claims, brand drift, and fidelity issues.

families.title: UGC Offering Pose Thumbnail
families.summary: A high-energy creator-style image where a person offers the subject close to the camera while copy and marker-like accents make the ad feel native to short-form social.
families.businessGoal: test whether the subject can feel human, direct, and recommendation-led without losing product fidelity
families.scene: a real-life interior or street-fashion environment with side light, tactile shadows, a wide-angle foreground subject, and the creator mid-ground with a vivid expression
families.focus: personal recommendation, immediacy, and product visibility
families.decision: whether a social-native human thumbnail should lead the first paid or creator test

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; place behind the person or subject for depth.
copyRules.supportingText: Use up to three short exact benefit tags.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA or contact detail only.

families.promptTemplate: Create a photorealistic UGC-style lifestyle ad thumbnail for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show one expressive [AUDIENCE] creator in a plausible real-life setting, holding the subject close to the camera in an offering pose. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] accents in wardrobe and decor. Integrate [HEADLINE] as large clean type with depth behind the person, add short handwritten tags only if supplied, and place [CTA_OR_CONTACT] in a small clean footer capsule. Preserve subject shape, label, logo, proportions, and claims exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: preserve product label and orientation
keep hands and fingers anatomically natural
do not invent demographics beyond the brief
do not add fake creator handles
keep benefit tags short and exact
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: extra hands, warped limbs, flipped labels, plastic skin, fake endorsements, illegible headline, product hidden by text

families.title: Surreal Y2K Floating Product Sculpture
families.summary: A glossy Y2K-inspired product cluster with inflated objects, floating type, saturated sky or studio depth, and playful category symbols.
families.businessGoal: test whether the subject can become an iconic sculptural hero with strong recall and playful social appeal
families.scene: a bright saturated world with floating product duplicates, glossy balloon-like support objects, puffy ribbons, bloom, rim light, and atmospheric depth
families.focus: product silhouette, playful abundance, and memorable visual identity
families.decision: whether a surreal render system should carry a drop, fashion, novelty, or youth-facing campaign
elements: single footer contact line when supplied

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline as giant 3D inflated or chrome lettering.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid extra labels unless supplied.
copyRules.cta: No button unless the brief explicitly asks for one.

families.promptTemplate: Create a surreal Y2K-inspired commercial 3D poster for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Float multiple readable versions of the subject as hero sculptures with glossy inflated supporting symbols tied to [PRIMARY_BENEFIT] and [BRAND_PALETTE]. Use radiant backlight, soft bloom, high-gloss reflections, and crisp product edges. Add [HEADLINE] as clean 3D inflated or chrome type. Add [CTA_OR_CONTACT] only if supplied. Preserve the subject exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: keep product readable and not mirrored
separate product material from balloon material
use support symbols that fit the category
do not add faces unless requested
keep text sparse
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: scorched texture, damaged product, melted logo, chaotic overlap, unreadable 3D type, duplicate unsupplied logos

families.title: Luxury Editorial Still Life
families.summary: A restrained high-end still life using premium surfaces, negative space, material contrast, and exacting light control.
families.businessGoal: make the subject feel expensive, considered, and visually ownable without overexplaining the offer
families.scene: a precise studio or architectural still-life setup with marble, lacquer, brushed metal, linen, glass, or other category-appropriate materials
families.focus: craft, finish, taste, and premium recognition
families.decision: whether a premium editorial route fits the category and audience

copyRules.headline: Use either no headline or a small exact editorial headline.
copyRules.supportingText: Keep body text as placeholder lines unless supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a luxury editorial still-life advertisement for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Place the subject on a refined [BRAND_PALETTE]-compatible surface with disciplined negative space, controlled reflections, and one restrained prop family that supports [PRIMARY_BENEFIT]. Use magazine-quality lighting and exact product preservation. Add only exact supplied text. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: preserve label, shape, material, and scale
avoid stock-photo generic props
keep composition quiet
do not invent awards or premium claims
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: busy prop styling, fake luxury logos, unreadable microtext, distorted reflections, unrelated jewelry or fragrance cues

families.title: Hard Sell Direct Response Card
families.summary: A clear conversion ad with product, headline, proof slot, benefit stack, CTA, and urgency space when approved.
families.businessGoal: test whether a straightforward conversion layout communicates the offer quickly and reviewably
families.scene: a clean high-contrast ad card with product hero, benefit hierarchy, proof or rating placeholder, CTA block, and channel-safe margins
families.focus: offer clarity and fast comprehension
families.decision: whether the subject should be sold through a direct response angle

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use exact approved benefits or abstract placeholder lines.

families.promptTemplate: Create a polished direct-response image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Build a clear conversion card with product hero, [HEADLINE], up to three exact approved benefit bullets, proof placeholder only if supplied, and [CTA_OR_CONTACT] as a clean button or footer. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] for hierarchy and contrast. Preserve all subject facts. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent discounts, reviews, prices, or ratings
keep the layout readable at feed size
use deterministic-looking text zones
preserve product exactly
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake countdowns, fake star ratings, too many claims, noisy badges, broken text hierarchy

families.title: Problem Solution Split Screen
families.summary: A two-panel ad that contrasts a problem context with the improved state enabled by the subject while avoiding exaggerated claims.
families.businessGoal: make the need-state and the product role understandable without relying on long copy
families.scene: a clean split image with left-side friction, right-side resolved moment, and the subject clearly bridging or anchoring the improvement
families.focus: what changes for the buyer or user
families.decision: whether contrast-based messaging is appropriate for the offer

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline or no headline.
copyRules.supportingText: Use neutral labels such as Before and After only when supplied or appropriate.

families.promptTemplate: Create a problem-solution split-screen advertisement for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show the problem state on the left and the improved state on the right, with the subject clearly preserved and central to the change. Use neutral, believable visual contrast and [BRAND_PALETTE]. Add exact supplied labels only. Avoid exaggerated outcomes and [AVOID].
constraints: avoid medical, financial, or performance claims unless approved
make both sides plausible
do not shame users
keep the subject visible
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fearmongering, unrealistic transformation, fake metrics, aggressive red-green cliches, unreadable labels

families.title: Cinematic Outdoor Action Hero
families.humanPresence: one or more users when relevant
families.summary: A dramatic action scene where the subject is used in motion with environmental energy, scale, and premium cinematography.
families.businessGoal: test whether the subject can credibly own an active performance or adventure moment
families.scene: a real outdoor or urban environment with directional light, motion cues, environmental texture, and the subject clearly visible in use
families.focus: capability, momentum, and aspiration
families.decision: whether active-use storytelling should lead the campaign
elements: motion trails or natural action cues

copyRules.headline: Use no headline or one exact short headline in open space.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid benefit tags unless supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a cinematic outdoor action advertisement for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show the subject credibly in use by [AUDIENCE] or staged as the hero in an active environment. Use strong natural light, movement cues, atmospheric depth, and [BRAND_PALETTE] accents. Preserve the subject exactly and add only exact supplied text. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: keep action physically plausible
do not obscure the subject
avoid unsafe usage
respect real-world scale
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: extreme stunt fantasy, product motion blur, impossible body positions, generic adventure stock look

families.title: App UI Holographic Overlay
families.summary: A technology ad that shows the subject with clean floating UI layers, workflow indicators, or feature modules without inventing product screens.
families.businessGoal: make a digital or connected product feel tangible while keeping UI claims controlled
families.scene: a dark or bright technical environment with the subject foregrounded and translucent interface cards arranged in perspective
families.focus: feature clarity, control, and modern utility
families.decision: whether UI-led visual explanation should be used before a deterministic product mockup is built

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use placeholder UI blocks unless real UI copy is supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a premium technology image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE] with holographic UI overlays. Preserve the actual product, app, or device boundaries exactly. Show translucent feature cards and workflow indicators that imply [PRIMARY_BENEFIT] without inventing real UI copy. Use [BRAND_PALETTE], controlled glow, and clean composition. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent product interface claims
keep UI text abstract unless supplied
do not distort screens
avoid cybersecurity or AI claims unless approved
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake dashboards, random code, unreadable neon clutter, overdone sci-fi effects, wrong device OS

families.title: Exploded View Benefit Diagram
families.summary: An exploded or cutaway ad that visualizes components, ingredients, layers, or service parts as a clear benefit story.
families.businessGoal: turn product construction or service logic into a concise visual proof system
families.scene: a clean studio or technical diagram space where subject layers float apart with callout lines and disciplined labels
families.focus: how the subject works or why it is different
families.decision: whether the offer needs a demo-led ad rather than a lifestyle ad

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline or no headline.
copyRules.supportingText: Use numbered placeholders unless real feature names are supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create an exploded-view benefit diagram ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Separate the subject into plausible layers, components, ingredients, or service steps only when supported by the brief. Use clean callouts, numbered markers, and [BRAND_PALETTE]. Preserve the product form and avoid inventing internal mechanisms. Add exact supplied text only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not show false internal parts
label assumptions as placeholders
keep callouts sparse
preserve outer product recognition
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake engineering claims, excessive labels, impossible cross-sections, medical diagrams for nonmedical products

families.title: Retail Shelf Battle Card
families.summary: A shelf or marketplace-style image that tests whether the subject stands out in a buying environment while avoiding fake competitor brands.
families.businessGoal: evaluate shelf clarity, category fit, and shopper recognition
families.scene: a clean retail shelf, endcap, marketplace grid, or display context with the subject repeated or heroed at realistic scale
families.focus: visibility, pack read, and purchase readiness
families.decision: whether retail or ecommerce context should be prioritized for the campaign

copyRules.headline: Use no headline or exact retail message.
copyRules.supportingText: Use generic shelf tags unless supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a retail shelf-impact ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Place the subject in a plausible shelf, endcap, or ecommerce grid context with generic category neighbors and clean purchase cues. Preserve packaging, scale, and label hierarchy. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] for display accents. Add exact supplied text only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent retailer names
do not create competitor lookalikes
keep price and promo tags generic unless supplied
preserve product hierarchy
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake retailer branding, chaotic shelf clutter, wrong product category, unreadable repeated facings

families.title: Minimal Premium Whitespace
families.summary: A spare, high-control image where the subject and a single line of exact copy carry the ad.
families.businessGoal: test whether the brand can communicate confidence through restraint
families.scene: a clean white, off-white, or muted color field with the subject precisely placed, elegant shadow, and generous negative space
families.focus: clarity, restraint, and product confidence
families.decision: whether a minimal premium system is strong enough for the category

copyRules.headline: Use one exact short line only.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid supporting copy.
copyRules.cta: Use a small exact CTA only if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a minimal premium image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Use a clean field, precise product placement, refined shadow, and generous negative space. Add [HEADLINE] only if supplied, set with quiet premium typography. Preserve the subject exactly and avoid decorative clutter. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not fill the space with props
keep product scale deliberate
use exact text only
avoid generic tech gradients
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: floating orbs, random abstract shapes, tiny product, over-styled typography, missing subject detail

families.title: Maximalist Collage Scrapbook
families.summary: A layered collage ad with cutouts, stickers, tape, snapshots, scribbles, swatches, and product repeats arranged with intentional energy.
families.businessGoal: test a playful high-information route for audiences that respond to taste cues and social proof texture
families.scene: a tabletop, wall, or digital scrapbook composition layered with product cutouts, lifestyle fragments, stickers, notes, and palette cues
families.focus: abundance, personality, and shareable campaign texture
families.decision: whether the campaign should lean social, expressive, and collected rather than polished and minimal

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline as one dominant cutout or sticker.
copyRules.supportingText: Use only supplied short notes or placeholder marks.
copyRules.cta: Small exact CTA sticker if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a maximalist collage scrapbook image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Build layered product cutouts, lifestyle fragments, stickers, tape, texture, and [BRAND_PALETTE] swatches around [PRIMARY_BENEFIT]. Keep the subject recognizable in every repeat. Use exact supplied copy only and preserve product details. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: keep at least one clean product hero
do not hide labels under stickers
avoid copyrighted character-like stickers
control visual hierarchy
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: messy moodboard filler, illegible sticker text, too many product duplicates, unrelated pop-culture references

families.title: Cinematic Noir Spotlight
families.summary: A dramatic low-key ad using spotlight, haze, shadow, and controlled color to make the subject feel consequential.
families.businessGoal: test whether mystery, tension, and premium drama can elevate the subject
families.scene: a dark stage, alley, studio, or architectural set with one precise beam of light, haze, reflective surfaces, and a restrained headline zone
families.focus: intrigue, focus, and premium tension
families.decision: whether a cinematic teaser route fits the brand and category

copyRules.headline: Use one exact teaser line or no text.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid explanatory copy.
copyRules.cta: Omit or use very small exact CTA.

families.promptTemplate: Create a cinematic noir spotlight ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Place the subject in a dark dramatic environment with one controlled light source, deep shadow, haze, and [BRAND_PALETTE] accent highlights. Use sparse exact copy only. Preserve the subject shape, material, and label. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: keep subject readable despite darkness
avoid horror cues unless requested
do not invent film titles or awards
control reflections
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: muddy underexposure, fake movie billing blocks, unreadable product, excessive smoke, dangerous usage

families.title: Cold Splash Flavor Word Backdrop
families.summary: A high-speed refreshment ad built around a centered cold product, visible condensation, giant flavor-word typography, citrus or ingredient cues, and clean cool color.
families.businessGoal: make refreshment or freshness instantly legible
families.scene: a clean studio setup with the product centered over bold capital flavor text, surrounded by truthful fruit, herb, ice, droplets, splash arcs, condensation, and blue-toned coolness cues
families.focus: coldness, flavor, immediacy, and crisp refreshment
families.decision: whether a high-sensory motion route fits the product and season

copyRules.headline: Use one exact short headline if supplied.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid body copy.

families.promptTemplate: Create a cold splash flavor-word image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Center the product sharply over a huge bold capital flavor word or headline from [HEADLINE], with condensation, water droplets, crushed ice, splash motion, and truthful fruit or herb cues that support [PRIMARY_BENEFIT]. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] plus cool blue freshness accents as appropriate. Preserve label, form, and orientation exactly. Do not copy another beverage brand's identity unless that brand is the supplied subject. Add exact supplied text only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: use only plausible flavor or ingredient cues
keep label readable
do not mimic Sprite or another beverage brand unless supplied
avoid unsafe glass or liquid chaos
do not invent health claims
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: label covered by water, fake fruit flavors, over-saturated color, product crushed or damaged

families.title: Cozy Home Ritual
families.summary: A warm domestic ad where the subject appears in a believable routine with tactile comfort, daylight, and household trust.
families.businessGoal: connect the subject to habit, comfort, repeat use, and familiarity
families.scene: a kitchen, bedroom, living room, bathroom, or entryway routine with warm natural light, useful props, and the subject placed in active context
families.focus: habit, care, warmth, and practical desirability
families.decision: whether a home-use moment should anchor the campaign

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline if it fits the scene.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid dense text.

families.promptTemplate: Create a cozy home ritual image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Place the subject in a believable household routine for [AUDIENCE], using warm daylight, tactile surfaces, and useful props that support [PRIMARY_BENEFIT]. Preserve the subject exactly and use [BRAND_PALETTE] accents naturally. Add exact supplied text only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: make the routine plausible
avoid staged stock-photo smiles
do not add unsupported family or health claims
keep product visible
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: generic beige lifestyle scene, cluttered home, unsafe product use, unapproved children or pets

families.title: Streetwear Drop Poster
families.summary: A high-contrast fashion or youth-culture ad with street textures, product scale, bold type, and limited-release energy.
families.businessGoal: test scarcity, attitude, and street-level desirability without inventing false availability claims
families.scene: an urban wall, studio floor, alley, skate spot, or backstage setup with gritty texture, fashion lighting, and product-forward composition
families.focus: identity, drop energy, and visual memorability
families.decision: whether the campaign should use street-fashion codes

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied drop name or headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use dates or scarcity language only if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a streetwear drop poster image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Use gritty urban texture, fashion lighting, bold type, and [BRAND_PALETTE] accents to communicate [PRIMARY_BENEFIT] or drop energy. Add only exact supplied drop copy, dates, or CTA. Preserve product form and graphics exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent limited quantities or dates
avoid direct brand mimicry
keep product graphics readable
make typography intentional
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake collaboration logos, counterfeit luxury cues, unreadable graffiti, product covered by stickers

families.title: Out Of Home City Takeover
families.summary: A public-space ad visualization showing the campaign on billboards, transit shelters, kiosks, or building wraps with realistic scale.
families.businessGoal: test whether the ad idea has enough simplicity and scale for out-of-home placement
families.scene: a city street, transit station, airport corridor, or retail district with one or more large ad placements integrated into real architecture
families.focus: public readability, brand scale, and campaign presence
families.decision: whether the concept can graduate to OOH planning

copyRules.headline: Use one exact short headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid long copy.
copyRules.cta: Use URL or CTA only if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a photoreal out-of-home city takeover mockup for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Integrate the campaign image into billboards, transit shelters, kiosks, or building wraps in a plausible public environment. Keep the ad simple, readable, and aligned to [BRAND_PALETTE]. Use exact supplied copy only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not imply real media placement
avoid real city landmarks unless supplied
keep text readable at distance
do not invent permits or partner logos
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: busy tiny text, impossible billboard geometry, fake event sponsorship, distorted ad panels

families.title: Ecommerce Bundle Hero
families.summary: A clean ecommerce ad showing the subject, bundle contents, scale, variants, or included items with high shopping clarity.
families.businessGoal: make the offer easy to understand at purchase point
families.scene: a bright product layout with hero item, optional bundle components, variant cues, included accessories, and clean benefit icons or placeholders
families.focus: what is included, what it looks like, and why it is worth buying
families.decision: whether ecommerce clarity should be prioritized over lifestyle storytelling

copyRules.headline: Use exact product or offer name only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use exact included-item labels only.

families.promptTemplate: Create an ecommerce bundle hero ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Arrange the product, included items, variants, or accessories in a clean shoppable layout with [BRAND_PALETTE], realistic shadows, and high product clarity. Use exact supplied item labels only. Preserve package, logo, and proportions exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent bundle contents
keep product count accurate
avoid lifestyle clutter
make all items legible
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake accessories, impossible scale, marketplace badges, distorted packaging, made-up discounts

families.title: Testimonial Proof Wall
families.humanPresence: optional real customer portrait only if supplied
families.summary: A proof-led ad that visualizes reviews, quotes, customer logos, or outcome snippets as a structured wall without inventing evidence.
families.businessGoal: show credibility and momentum using approved proof assets
families.scene: a clean wall, dashboard, board, or editorial layout with subject hero and proof cards arranged around it
families.focus: trust, validation, and repeated positive signal
families.decision: whether proof should lead the ad instead of product beauty or lifestyle

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied proof headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use only real supplied quotes, names, logos, or abstract placeholders.

families.promptTemplate: Create a testimonial proof wall image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Arrange approved quote cards, review snippets, customer proof, or placeholder proof modules around the subject. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] and clear hierarchy. Do not invent customers, ratings, logos, or metrics. Use exact supplied proof only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: never invent endorsements
use placeholders when proof is missing
avoid fake profile photos
keep claims reviewable
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake five-star reviews, made-up customer logos, fabricated metrics, dense unreadable quote walls

families.title: Founder Operator Desk Ad
families.humanPresence: one founder, operator, clinician, expert, or employee when appropriate
families.summary: An expert or operator-led ad where a credible person works with the subject in a real workspace, counter, lab, studio, or field context.
families.businessGoal: make the offer feel expert-backed, operational, and grounded in real use
families.scene: a desk, workshop, clinic, kitchen, studio, lab, or field workspace with the person mid-task and the subject clearly placed as the tool or offer
families.focus: credibility, craft, and hands-on use
families.decision: whether human expertise should anchor the campaign

copyRules.headline: Use exact quote or headline only.
copyRules.supportingText: Do not name a person unless supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create an operator-led editorial image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show a credible [AUDIENCE] expert or operator using or presenting the subject in a real workspace. Use natural light, honest texture, and [BRAND_PALETTE] accents. Add exact supplied quote or headline only. Preserve the subject exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent names, credentials, or employers
avoid fake lab or medical authority
keep workspace relevant
show the subject clearly
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: stock handshake energy, staged corporate cliches, false expertise, over-polished office scenes

families.title: Retro Print Classified
families.summary: A vintage print ad route using halftone, catalog layouts, coupons, serif type, linework, and aged paper texture while preserving modern product truth.
families.businessGoal: test whether nostalgia and print credibility can make the offer feel familiar and memorable
families.scene: a newspaper, catalog page, coupon card, or magazine spread with the subject rendered or photographed cleanly and surrounded by period-inspired layout
families.focus: nostalgia, clarity, and distinctive print texture
families.decision: whether a retro route offers enough differentiation for the audience

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline in period-style type.
copyRules.supportingText: Use placeholder body copy unless supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use coupon or URL only if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a retro print classified image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Use vintage newspaper, catalog, or coupon layout cues with halftone texture, serif typography, and [BRAND_PALETTE] adapted to aged print. Preserve the product exactly and use exact supplied copy only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: avoid offensive or exclusionary vintage stereotypes
do not add fake prices or coupon codes
keep product modern and accurate
make text intentionally sparse
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: unreadable tiny newspaper copy, fake legal terms, caricatured people, distressed product

families.title: Sticker Badge Social Native
families.summary: A social-native layout with the subject surrounded by crisp stickers, badges, emoji-like marks, and short callouts that feel casual but controlled.
families.businessGoal: test a playful platform-native route while preserving approved copy and product fidelity
families.scene: a clean product or lifestyle image overlaid with sticker badges, reaction marks, arrows, and short labels in brand colors
families.focus: platform-native attention and quick benefit cues
families.decision: whether the ad should look like native creator content rather than a formal brand asset

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied short headline or sticker callout.
copyRules.supportingText: Use no more than four short exact callouts.

families.promptTemplate: Create a sticker-badge social-native image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Keep the subject clear, then add crisp sticker badges, arrows, reaction marks, and short exact callouts in [BRAND_PALETTE]. Make it casual and platform-native without adding fake handles, reviews, or claims. Preserve the subject exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not use copyrighted emoji or platform UI
keep stickers off product labels
limit callouts
avoid fake social metrics
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: cluttered sticker spam, unreadable labels, fake likes or comments, childish tone for serious categories

families.title: World Extension Product Fantasy
families.summary: A surreal world where the subject's material, pattern, flavor, ingredient, or feature becomes a landscape or architecture.
families.businessGoal: test whether the product can support a distinctive campaign world
families.scene: an immersive landscape, room, city, terrain, or miniature world generated from the subject's colors, material, texture, or benefit metaphor
families.focus: brand world, memorability, and ownable metaphor
families.decision: whether to build a larger visual system around the subject
elements: subject as hero or landscape source

copyRules.headline: Use no text or one exact campaign line.
copyRules.supportingText: Avoid labels.

families.promptTemplate: Create an immersive product-world image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Transform the subject's truthful material, color, texture, ingredient, or benefit metaphor into a surreal environment while keeping the actual subject visible and accurate. Use [BRAND_PALETTE], atmospheric depth, and copy-safe space. Add exact supplied text only. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: keep the metaphor tied to real subject cues
do not replace the product with a different object
avoid impossible claims
make the world inspectable
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: abstract blob landscapes, product lost in scene, unrelated fantasy creatures, generic AI dream texture

families.title: Sustainability Material Proof
families.humanPresence: optional hands or field worker if supplied
families.summary: A proof-oriented ad showing materials, sourcing, packaging, repairability, refill, or lifecycle cues without inventing environmental claims.
families.businessGoal: make responsible design or material choices tangible and reviewable
families.scene: a clean material board, sourcing table, workshop, farm, lab, or refill station with the subject and documented material cues
families.focus: trust, material detail, and responsible choice
families.decision: whether sustainability or material proof should be part of the campaign

copyRules.headline: Use exact approved claim only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use placeholders for certifications unless supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a sustainability material proof image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show the subject with truthful material, sourcing, refill, repair, packaging, or lifecycle cues from the brief. Use documentation-style composition, [BRAND_PALETTE], and exact approved claims only. Do not invent certifications. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not imply carbon, recycled, organic, or ethical claims unless supplied
label unknown proof as placeholder
keep materials relevant
avoid greenwashing imagery
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: fake leaves and badges, unsupported eco claims, invented certifications, vague planet-saving slogans

families.title: Event Launch Countdown
families.humanPresence: optional speaker or audience if supplied
families.summary: A launch or event ad with the subject, date zone, agenda cue, countdown energy, and clean registration CTA.
families.businessGoal: turn a release, webinar, demo, or event into a clear visual invitation
families.scene: a stage, digital backdrop, calendar-like graphic, product reveal setup, or event card with clear date and CTA zones
families.focus: what is happening, when, and why to act
families.decision: whether the subject needs an event-led campaign wrapper

copyRules.headline: Use exact event name only.
copyRules.supportingText: Use date, time, speaker, and agenda only if supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create an event launch countdown image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Build a polished event key visual with exact supplied event name, date, time, agenda, and [CTA_OR_CONTACT]. Use [BRAND_PALETTE], clear hierarchy, and a credible reveal or stage environment. Preserve the subject exactly. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not invent event date, speaker, or venue
keep date text large and exact
avoid fake conference logos
preserve product or event identity
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: wrong calendar info, generic webinar stock art, fake speaker portraits, unreadable agenda text

families.title: Cinematic Hands Demo
families.summary: A close-up image showing hands using, opening, applying, assembling, scanning, pouring, or demonstrating the subject.
families.businessGoal: make use and interaction concrete before video or full demo production
families.scene: a close working surface, counter, desk, bathroom, kitchen, retail counter, or field setup with hands naturally interacting with the subject
families.focus: how it is used and why it feels easy or satisfying
families.decision: whether a use-moment demo should anchor the ad

copyRules.headline: Use exact short headline if supplied.
copyRules.supportingText: Use step labels only when supplied.

families.promptTemplate: Create a cinematic hands-demo image ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Show natural hands using, opening, applying, assembling, or demonstrating the subject in a plausible setting for [AUDIENCE]. Use close-up lighting, tactile detail, and [BRAND_PALETTE] accents. Preserve subject shape, labels, and usage truth. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: hands must be anatomically natural
use only safe and accurate interactions
do not invent use steps
keep product label visible when relevant
do not add subtitles, body-copy captions, explanatory footer text, or small descriptive text unless the user supplied exact copy for it
families.avoid: extra fingers, wrong usage, messy workspace, product obscured by hands, fake app screens

review.title: Ads Explorer
review.note: Image-led review wall. Captions and prompt details stay in the manifest, not under the tiles.
